ideation: This animation sequence depicts the robot carefully tying a knot, emphasizing the precision and concentration involved.  The movement will focus on the hands coming together, wrapping around each other, and then settling into a final tied position.  The sequence is designed to be slow and deliberate, highlighting the detailed actions involved in knot-tying.  The left arm will primarily perform the wrapping action, while the right arm will provide support and stability.  Both arms use joint1 and joint2 to create a natural, flowing motion that avoids jerky movements. The wrists are used to adjust the hand orientations for optimal grip and knot-formation.

frames.beat: arms held out, ready to begin
hands cross and begin to wrap the rope
fingers twist and tighten the forming knot
the knot is pulled snug, the task complete
